children.heading: .—Unit of general local government
children.label: 1 .—Unit of general local government
children.text: The term “unit of general local government” means a city, county, town, parish, village, or other general-purpose political subdivision of a State.

children.label: A
children.text: a geographical area within the jurisdiction of an incorporated city, town, borough, village, or other unit of general local government, except a county or parish, having a population of at least 10,000 inhabitants;

children.label: B
children.text: that portion of the geographical area within the jurisdiction of a county, town, township, or similar governmental entity which contains no incorporated unit of general local government but has a population density of at least 1,500 inhabitants per square mile; and

children.label: C
children.text: that portion of a geographical area having a population density of at least 1,500 inhabitants per square mile and situated adjacent to the boundary of an incorporated unit of general local government which has a population of at least 10,000.
